title: News & Current Events Vocabulary
description: Understand and talk about news, media, and current events with critical vocabulary.

items.example: The headline read: 'Scientists Discover New Planet'.
The foreign correspondent reported live from the conflict zone.

items.front: to report / to cover / to investigate
items.example: The journalist covered the story for three months before publishing.
According to officials, the situation is under control.

items.back: noticias de última hora / actualizaciones en directo
items.example: Breaking news: a major earthquake has struck the coast.
Fact: The temperature rose 2°C. Opinion: This will be catastrophic.
Always verify information before sharing it online.
This headline is clickbait — the article says something completely different.
Only share news from reliable, credible sources.

items.prompt: The ___ of the article read: 'Economy Set to Crash — Experts Warn.'
items.explanation: A 'headline' is the title of a news article, designed to grab the reader's attention.

items.prompt: The journalist spent six months ___ the corruption scandal before publishing.
items.explanation: 'Investigating' = digging into a story to find the truth. This took six months of deep research.

items.prompt: ___ to government officials, the situation is under control.
items.explanation: 'According to + source' = a way of attributing information without confirming it yourself.

items.prompt: The headline promised a shocking story but the article was totally different. This is called ___.
items.explanation: 'Clickbait' = content designed to attract clicks with a misleading or exaggerated headline.

items.prompt: Before sharing a story, you should always ___ the information with multiple sources.
items.explanation: 'Verify' = check that something is true. Always verify before sharing news online.

items.sentence: The journalist ___ the story live from the disaster zone for three days.
This article lacks any credible ___ — I can't tell where the information comes from.
___ to witnesses, the accident happened at approximately 3 pm.
This is breaking ___! An agreement has just been signed.
Never share content without first ___ that the information is accurate.
